subject: [PATCH v1 8/9] fuse: add zero-copy over io-uring

2026-03-24.patch_summary: This patch adds support for zero-copy data transfer over io-uring for the FUSE file system. It allows privileged servers to preregister a kernel-managed buffer ring, a registered memory region, and a sparse buffer corresponding to the queue depth. This enables the elimination of memory copies between the kernel and userspace for read/write operations, resulting in improved performance. Benchmarks show approximately 20-25% increases in throughput for certain workloads. The patch modifies several files in the FUSE codebase to implement this feature.
